rationale_en: Export-credit insurance de-risks exporting but is one of many small trade-finance tools against total merchandise exports, dominated by oil-linked flows.
Defines liability of officials executing divestiture deals, a procedural insurance detail with no channel to market capitalization.
Commission rates are a real driver of agent and broker recruitment, but network size is also shaped by licensing rules and insurer strategy.
A narrow disability-housing benefit is too small a slice of national social-protection coverage to move the aggregate percentage.
A bilateral investment treaty with a minor capital-source country like Qatar has negligible expected weight against aggregate FDI inflows.
A bilateral investment treaty with a small partner like Tunisia has negligible expected weight against aggregate FDI inflows.
A double-taxation treaty with a small trade partner like Armenia is a minor legal nicety with no material effect on FDI or tax revenue aggregates.
A regional trade-liberalization pact among ECO members is a real but marginal force against Iran's total merchandise trade flows.
A bilateral trade agreement with a major neighbor like Turkey has a real but partial effect, one instrument among many shaping trade volumes.
A bilateral trade agreement with a small partner like Syria is too minor to expect a visible effect on aggregate trade flows.
A customs-enforcement cooperation pact targets smuggling compliance, not trade volume, and has no expected macro effect on exports or imports.
A court ruling on a single municipality's local levy on wells has no plausible channel to national oil production volume.
Land Reform is central to any account of the Shah-era growth story, but is one of several forces alongside oil revenue and industrial policy driving GDP.
The law directly restructured land tenure and cultivation incentives, the core structural content of these agriculture categories.
Redistributing land from a landlord class to smallholders was a real rural income shock, but national inequality also reflects urban and oil-rent dynamics.
One year's national budget is a modest force against the full economy, one of many annual budget laws over the GDP series.
This is the direct legal instrument that set government spending and revenue for the fiscal year, the definitional source of the government-finance series.
One early budget law is a modest force against the whole economy despite its symbolic founding-era significance.
This is the direct legal instrument that fixed government expenditure and revenue for that fiscal year, defining the government-finance series itself.
The Mehr programme was a mass residential construction drive, a real contributor to fixed capital formation but one component of a broad investment aggregate.
Implicit interest-rate subsidies on Mehr housing credit are a marginal component of the subsidies and transfers line, dominated by energy and bread subsidies.
This mechanism directly removed the main FX-risk barrier deterring private power investment, a major instrument against this narrow, targeted measure.
A single-sector FX conversion scheme for power producers has no expected channel to the aggregate USD exchange rate.
A single 150-billion-rial bond tranche is negligible against the stock of government debt to the central bank.
A narrow amendment to one note under one clause of a trade bylaw is a procedural tweak with no expected effect on trade volumes.
A single added article to the trade bylaw is a narrow procedural addition, not a policy shift with expected effect on trade flows.
A single added clause to one article of the trade bylaw is a narrow procedural tweak with no expected effect on trade volumes.
An early foundational income-tax regulation had real historical effect but was long since superseded by later tax law, diluting its weight on modern tax-revenue statistics.
A narrow technical-standard amendment for vehicle imports is a minor regulatory tweak, not a major driver of domestic production volume.
A routine periodic electricity-tariff circular is a real administered-price input to CPI and WPI, but one component among many, not a landmark nationwide price reset.
Higher regulated tariffs can dampen consumption growth at the margin, but usage is driven far more by economic activity and demographics.
A duplicate periodic electricity-tariff circular is a real but partial administered-price input to CPI and WPI.
Higher regulated tariffs can dampen consumption growth at the margin, but usage is driven far more by economic activity and demographics.
Transmitting one list of exempt medical import orders is a narrow administrative step within a subsidy mechanism, not a policy change with aggregate weight.
A narrow medical-import exemption list is too small an administrative detail to move aggregate government health-expenditure share.
A duplicate narrow administrative list-transmission circular has no material weight on aggregate subsidy transfers.
A duplicate narrow administrative list-transmission circular has no material weight on health-expenditure share.
Earmarking one narrow revenue line within a single year's budget is a procedural allocation with negligible weight against total budget figures.
A cotton procurement committee could plausibly influence administered prices, but this is an organizational bylaw, not itself a price-setting act, and one force among market conditions.
The link from a procurement committee's structure to planting decisions and output is indirect and diluted by weather and market conditions.
Land Reform is central to any account of the Shah-era growth story, but one of several forces alongside oil revenue and industrialization driving GDP.
The law directly restructured land tenure and cultivation incentives, the core structural content of these agriculture categories.
Breaking up large estates reduced landlord wealth concentration, a real force on top wealth and income shares, but rural-only and one force among several shaping national inequality.
The reform reshaped rural labor relations, but the long-run decline in agriculture's employment share owes as much to mechanization and urban migration.
This law created the modern regulatory architecture and securities regulator for Iran's capital markets, defining the very structure this category measures.
An AML framework is a real input to governance-quality perceptions, but one of many factors alongside sanctions and broader institutional conditions.
AML compliance is a minor consideration for investors against the sanctions and political risk that dominate Iran's FDI story.
This law renegotiated the terms under which Iranian crude was produced and revenue shared, a major instrument with expected material effect on oil output and rents.
Oil dominated 1970s state revenue, but a contract-terms renegotiation is a more indirect GDP driver than the oil-price shocks of the era.
The law shaped the volume and terms of crude sold through the consortium, a real if partial channel into the fuel share of exports already dominated by oil.

The ruling directly reversed a cut to the guaranteed vegetable-oil purchase price, a real effect on that specific commodity price though narrower against the broader agricultural producer-price aggregate.
Duty collection in free trade zones is a real but geographically narrow slice of national customs-duty revenue.
As the operational rulebook for licensing most foreign trade, this is a real structural driver, though wartime trade volumes were shaped as much by rationing and oil revenue.
Compulsory land purchases for industrial use are a real but narrow instrument reallocating land away from farming, not a general agricultural policy.
Land acquisition for industrial siting is a marginal, diffuse input to aggregate industrial value added, which is driven far more by capital and technology.
An explicit livestock-support law targets exactly these production series, but output is also driven by feed costs, disease, and drought, so the effect is real but partial.
Electrifying well pumps is a direct instrument improving irrigation reliability, though total irrigated land also depends on broader water infrastructure.
Electrifying agricultural wells extended the rural grid at the margin, but broader rural electrification programs are the larger driver of rural access.
A national urban-renewal framework is a real but partial policy input against housing-stock growth over decades, which is dominated by population growth and private construction.
Founding the industrial estates institution is a real but diffuse, long-run channel into aggregate industry value added, which is driven mostly by broader investment and sector mix.
As the entity physically siting industrial parks, this law has a more direct channel into the provincial distribution of workshops than into national aggregates.
Nationalizing one narrow subsector of contracting and consulting firms is too small a slice of the post-revolutionary economy to move aggregate GDP.
Contracting and engineering firms sit within the construction and industry sector, giving this narrow nationalization a real but partial effect on that sector's value added.
As a procedural follow-on governing subsequent land transfers, this law continues the earlier reform's tenure restructuring but with a smaller incremental effect than the original shock.
Dissolving the crown-lands agency transferred meaningful state landholdings into private hands, complementing land reform, but was a secondary component next to the main reform.
A coordinating council on employment policy has the right purpose but limited independent program or budget authority, so a visible effect on aggregate labor statistics is not expected.
Extending mandatory insurance to a large, previously informal occupational segment is a direct instrument for coverage, though enforcement gaps and other sectoral extensions also shape the aggregate.
Formalizing construction workers' insurance status changes their coverage status, not employment counts, so there is no expected channel to the industry employment share.
Resetting the annual permitted-goods and licensing list is a real structural driver of trade composition, though volumes are shaped as much by oil revenue and the exchange rate.
Updating the goods-classification and licensing regime is a real structural driver of trade composition, though volumes are shaped as much by oil revenue and the exchange rate.
A single 100-billion-rial bond tranche is negligible against the aggregate stock of government debt and budget totals.
A one-year subsidized-housing sales scheme is a small program against the decades-long, nationwide housing-stock series.
A one-year wartime subsidized-housing sales scheme is a small program against the decades-long, nationwide housing-stock series.
An export working-capital fund eases exporter liquidity at the margin, one of several small trade-finance tools rather than a major driver.
As an implementing bylaw of the Article 44 privatization drive, it is a major instrument feeding the wave of listings and share transfers that built Iran's capital markets.
Article 44 privatization is one of the central structural reforms of the 2000s-2010s, but its GDP effect is real and partial next to sanctions and oil price as larger drivers.
A wage-classification methodology is a real but narrow technical tool shaping wage-setting mechanics, not a macro labor-market policy.
A court ruling striking a single clause of a trade bylaw is a narrow legal correction with no expected effect on trade volumes.
A court ruling on one clause of a supplementary tariff bylaw is a narrow legal correction, not a rate change, with no expected effect on the energy domain.
Annulling a single tax-administration circular is a narrow procedural correction with no expected effect on aggregate tax revenue.
A routine customs tariff-code refresh is a purely administrative classification update with no expected effect on import volumes.
A duplicate routine customs tariff-code refresh is a purely administrative classification update with no expected effect on import volumes.
A duplicate routine customs tariff-code refresh is a purely administrative classification update with no expected effect on import volumes.
A duplicate routine customs tariff-code refresh is a purely administrative classification update with no expected effect on import volumes.
A duplicate routine customs tariff-code refresh is a purely administrative classification update with no expected effect on import volumes.
A narrow list-transmission circular for exempt medical imports is too small an administrative detail to move aggregate subsidy transfers or out-of-pocket spending.
This circular is the direct administrative instrument setting electricity tariffs, a real driver of consumption within the broader energy domain.
A duplicate electricity-tariff circular is the direct administrative instrument setting prices, a real driver of consumption within the broader energy domain.
This amendment to the veterans and workers share-transfer programme is a real but smaller-scale privatization channel than the later Article 44 wave.
This bylaw operationalized the cash-transfer mechanism that became the dominant component of Iran's subsidies and transfers line from 2010 onward, a structural break in the series.
The subsidy reform this bylaw implemented triggered a nationwide, one-time administered-price reset visible as a sharp jump across CPI expenditure groups.
The universal cash-transfer programme this bylaw operationalized became Iran's largest safety-net programme by coverage, a major and visible driver of these indicators.
The subsidy reform is a landmark national policy with a well-documented inflation jump, though its net GDP effect is more contested and shared with sanctions-era forces.
State assistance for a specified list of export goods is a small, narrow historical instrument against aggregate merchandise exports dominated by oil.
As the recurring legal vehicle for Iran's annual trade regime, this law is a real structural driver of licensing and tariffs, though volumes are shaped as much by oil revenue and sanctions.
A direct customs-duty exemption on a specific staple import is a major, targeted instrument with an expected material effect on this narrow commodity trade series.
Lower import costs from the duty exemption plausibly supported vegetable-oil consumption, though consumption is also shaped by income and competing food prices.
The exemption affects import-parity pricing pressure on domestic oilcrop prices, one of several price determinants alongside global prices and domestic supply.
A small standard-mark surcharge and half-percent export fee is a minor cost-side instrument with negligible weight on aggregate merchandise exports.
This law directly amends the very spending and revenue totals that this Annual Budget Law Totals chart tracks for that fiscal year.
